skill.name: competitor-trial-monitor
skill.description: Monitor competitor clinical trial progress and alert on market risks.
skill.summary: Automated tracking of competitor clinical trials with risk alerting and reporting.
skill.author: AIPOCH

security_audit.summary: After AI review, all 95 static findings are dismissed as false positives or legitimate documented usage. The static analyzer misidentified markdown code formatting as shell backtick execution (57 findings) and matched weak-cryptography patterns on unrelated data access and argparse code (24 findings). The skill has real but documented risk factors: HTTPS requests to ClinicalTrials.gov API, file writes to ~/.openclaw/competitor-trial-monitor/, and Python script execution. No malicious intent, obfuscated code, credential exfiltration, or prompt injection was found. The skill is safe to publish with disclosure of its standard risk factors.

dangerous_patterns.title: Network Access to External API
dangerous_patterns.description: Script makes HTTPS requests to ClinicalTrials.gov API v2 for fetching clinical trial data. This is core functionality of the skill and uses the legitimate public API endpoint. All requests use HTTPS.

dangerous_patterns.title: Filesystem Write to Home Directory
dangerous_patterns.description: Script writes watchlist and alert data to ~/.openclaw/competitor-trial-monitor/. This is standard CLI tool behavior. Only reads and writes within its own data directory.

dangerous_patterns.title: Hidden Directory Access in Home Directory
dangerous_patterns.description: SKILL.md documents data storage in ~/.openclaw/competitor-trial-monitor/ directory. The script creates and manages files within this directory only. Standard practice for user-level CLI tools.

content.user_title: Monitor competitor clinical trials and assess market risks
content.value_statement: Tracking competitor clinical trial progress manually is slow and hard to maintain. This skill automates monitoring of ClinicalTrials.gov, detects status changes, and produces structured risk reports with alerts.
actual_capabilities: Track clinical trial status changes for specified competitor drugs using NCT IDs
Detect key milestones including enrollment completion, data unblinding, and results publication
Generate structured risk reports with categorized alerts (medium, high, critical)
Maintain a persistent watchlist of monitored trials in local storage
Alert on high-risk events such as regulatory submissions and market approvals
limitations: Only monitors trials from ClinicalTrials.gov, not EU Clinical Trials Register or WHO ICTRP
Requires manual configuration of each target trial using its NCT identifier
Does not perform statistical analysis or predict trial outcomes
Alert accuracy depends on the timeliness of ClinicalTrials.gov data updates

use_cases.title: Pharmaceutical competitive intelligence
use_cases.description: A pharma analyst monitors competitor drug trials to identify emerging market threats and inform strategic planning.

use_cases.title: Biotech investment research
use_cases.description: An investor tracks clinical trial progress of portfolio companies to evaluate pipeline value and inform decisions.

use_cases.title: R&D portfolio risk management
use_cases.description: An R&D manager watches competitor pipelines to adjust internal development priorities and resource allocation.

prompt_templates.title: Add a competitor trial to the watchlist
prompt_templates.prompt: Add NCT05108922 for Pfizer PF-07321332 in COVID-19 to the trial watchlist.
prompt_templates.scenario: Adding a new clinical trial to begin monitoring

prompt_templates.title: View the current monitoring list
prompt_templates.prompt: List all trials currently being monitored.
prompt_templates.scenario: Checking which competitor trials are being tracked

prompt_templates.title: Scan for updates and generate risk report
prompt_templates.prompt: Scan all monitored trials for status changes and generate a risk report covering the last 30 days.
prompt_templates.scenario: Running a periodic review of competitor trial activity

prompt_templates.title: Investigate a specific competitor alert
prompt_templates.prompt: A competitor phase 3 trial for our target indication just completed. Find it in the watchlist, scan for updates, and summarize all resulting risk alerts.
prompt_templates.scenario: Responding to a detected market risk signal

output_examples.input: Add NCT05108922 for Pfizer PF-07321332 in COVID-19 to the watchlist, then scan for updates.
output: ✅ Added: A Study to Evaluate Safety...

output_examples.input: Generate a risk report for the last 30 days.
output: 📊 Competitive product clinical trial risk report (last 30 days)
The trial has been completed and results may be announced soon

best_practices: Verify each NCT ID on ClinicalTrials.gov before adding it to the watchlist to ensure accuracy.
Run scans at regular intervals such as every 24 hours to avoid missing critical status changes.
Review alert history periodically to track competitor risk patterns and trends over time.
anti_patterns: Do not add trials without confirming the NCT ID exists on ClinicalTrials.gov first.
Do not use this skill to make investment decisions without verifying the underlying data.
Do not rely on automated risk levels alone; always review actual trial details for context.

faq.question: What data source does this skill use?
faq.answer: It fetches trial data from the ClinicalTrials.gov API v2. All requests use HTTPS.

faq.question: How often should I scan for updates?
faq.answer: The default scan interval is 24 hours. You can adjust this in the config.json file.

faq.question: What risk levels does the skill report?
faq.answer: It reports three levels: medium (enrollment completion), high (data unblinding, results, regulatory submission), and critical (approval granted).

faq.question: Can I monitor trials from registries other than ClinicalTrials.gov?
faq.answer: No. The skill currently only supports the ClinicalTrials.gov API. EU and WHO registries are not supported.

faq.question: Where is my monitoring data stored?
faq.answer: Data is stored in the ~/.openclaw/competitor-trial-monitor/ directory on your local machine.

faq.question: What happens if the ClinicalTrials.gov API is unavailable?
faq.answer: The script reports the error and continues. Previously fetched data remains available in local storage.
